highlighter.jumpNotFound: Highlight not found on this page

notes.title: Quick notes
notes.p1: Every site has its own notes sheet. Open it with the round side button, from the popup, or with right-click → Notes.
notes.p2: Use the toolbar below the sheet to add text, stickers, link buttons and images.
notes.p3: You can also drag images straight onto the sheet. This works from your computer and from any website.
notes.p4: Drag items to move them. Double-click to edit. Right-click to change color, layer or delete.

pages.title: Pages and titles
pages.p1: A site can have several note pages. Switch between them with the arrows above the sheet.
pages.p2: Type a title for each page in the field next to the arrows.
pages.p3: The + button adds a new page. The trash button deletes the current one.

highlight.title: Text highlighter
highlight.p1: Select text on a page and press the small button that appears. Right-click → Highlight also works.
highlight.p2: Right-click a highlight to open its editor. There you can change color and style, write a note or delete it.
highlight.p3: Hover over a highlight to read its note.
highlight.sample: This is what a highlight looks like

list.title: Side buttons and the list
list.p1: The round side buttons open quick notes and the list of highlights. Drag them up or down to reposition.
list.p2: The list has text search, color and style filters, and a page or whole-site view.
list.p3: Click an entry to jump to its text. On long feeds press Keep searching to load older history.

manager.title: Saved notes
manager.p1: Settings contains a manager of all your sites with three tabs: Sites, Notes and Text.
manager.p2: Click an entry to open the site. Notes open automatically and highlights are located on the page.

data.title: Your data
data.p1: Everything is stored locally in your browser. Nothing ever leaves your device.
data.p2: Use Export to save a JSON backup. Import it on another device to move your notes.
